title: Anuragia quadrifolia
taxonomy: Anuragia quadrifolia belongs to the family Anuragiaceae, a family of fungi that was previously classified under the family Pyronemataceae. The genus Anuragia is a monotypic genus, containing only one species. This species is classified as a type of fungus, specifically a member of the class Pezizomycetes. Within this class, Anuragia quadrifolia is further classified as an ascomycete, which is a group of fungi characterized by their ascus spores. The exact classification of Anuragia quadrifolia may be subject to change as taxonomy and classification continue to evolve.

intro: Anuragia quadrifolia is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the family Anuragiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, offering various health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections.
